Beta-1.9.9.description:
# Diablo II Archipelago — Beta 1.9.9

*Dev cycle in progress. Patch notes accumulate here as bug fixes land.*

---

## Install / update

(Filled in at release time.)

---

## What 1.9.9 fixes

### Custom Goal now generates the right number of locations

When you picked **Goal = Custom** with a narrow target (for example
"kill Andariel on Normal only"), the apworld used to generate the full
~700-location pool spanning all 5 acts and all 3 difficulties. In
single-player that was just wasteful — you'd never visit those NM/Hell
zones. In **multiworld** it was actively broken: other players'
progression items could get placed at your out-of-scope locations, and
when you completed your goal, those items stayed stuck until the host
ran `!release` manually.

**1.9.9 fix:** the apworld now computes the seed scope from the union
of the Custom Goal toggles you actually selected, and only generates
locations for difficulties and acts your character will reach.

Concrete impact (single-slot generation, no other options changed):

| Custom Goal selection                       | Pre-1.9.9 | 1.9.9 |
|---|---|---|
| `kill_andariel_normal` only                  | ~700      |  70   |
| `kill_baal_normal` only                      | ~700      | 223   |
| `subsystem_story_normal` only                | ~700      | 223   |
| `kill_diablo_nightmare` only                 | ~700      | 446   |
| `kill_andariel_normal + kill_baal_hell`      | ~700      | 668   |
| `kill_baal_hell` only                        | ~700      | 668   |
| Empty Custom Goal (no toggles)               | ~700      |  70   |

The 668-location case is the intended "full" scope — when your toggle
selection actually requires Hell access, the full pool is correct.
Multi-difficulty goals automatically force the full 5-act pool on
lower difficulties because D2 hard-codes act/diff transitions on
beating Baal (so you physically traverse the full game on Normal +
Nightmare before reaching anything on Hell).

**Multiworld safety:** with the scope shrunk to what you'll actually
visit, other players' items can no longer get placed at locations you
have no reason to visit. `!release` is no longer a mandatory step
after winning a narrow Custom Goal.

### Goal = Custom can now actually complete (was broken since 1.9.2)

A separate, more critical bug: the DLL's settings parser at
`d2arch_ap.c:912` was mapping `goal=4` (Custom) to `g_apGoal = 4 % 3 = 1`
(Full Nightmare). This meant `CustomGoal_IsComplete()` — the function
that ANDs your selected sub-targets together and triggers the AP win
signal — could **never fire** in AP mode. Players using Custom Goal
since 1.9.2 (when it shipped) were silently being given a Full
Nightmare seed regardless of what custom targets they picked. The
goal showed as "Custom" in the F1 menu but completion ran on Baal NM.

The 1.9.9 fix adds explicit `goal=4` handling to the parser:
```c
if (ival == 3)       g_apGoal = 3;          /* Collection mode */
else if (ival == 4)  g_apGoal = 4;          /* Custom Goal mode (NEW) */
else                 g_apGoal = (ival <= 2) ? ival : (ival % 3);
```

Combined with the scope fix above and the Pandemonium event wiring
below, Custom Goal is now fully functional end-to-end for the first
time since it was added.

### Pandemonium uber kills now register for Custom Goal

The DLL functions that record uber kills toward Custom Goal targets
existed since 1.9.2 (`CustomGoal_OnUberKilled`,
`CustomGoal_OnHellfireTorchComplete`) but had no callers — they were
dead code. So if you picked `kill_uber_mephisto`, `kill_uber_diablo`,
`kill_uber_baal`, or `hellfire_torch_complete` as Custom Goal targets,
they could never fire even if you killed those bosses in-game.

The 1.9.9 fix wires these into `Ubers_OnUnitDeathScan` in
`d2arch_ubers.c`:
- Each finale uber kill fires its corresponding Custom Goal bit
- The full Pandemonium completion (all 3 ubers + Torch drop) fires
  the `hellfire_torch_complete` bit

### Planned for this cycle (TODO)

(Move items from "Planned" to a real "## Bug fixes" section as they land.)

---

## Known issues — still under investigation

These bugs need user-side info (screenshots/logs) before we can fix:
- **Game crash after reaching Act 3 in AP mode** (Teddie) — multiple
  suspected causes; awaiting `d2arch.log` from a crash event
- **Skill page no info display** (Maegis) — three plausible causes,
  awaiting F1 tooltip screenshot
- **Champion enemies no names** (Maegis) — three plausible causes,
  awaiting screenshot showing whether prefix or basename is missing

---

*Build artifacts will be listed at release time. Cycle started 2026-05-13
on top of Beta 1.9.8 baseline.*
Beta-1.9.9.title: Beta 1.9.9

Stable-2.0.0.description:
# Diablo II Archipelago — Stable 2.0.0

The first **Stable** release. Diablo II Archipelago now runs through the new **Multiworld Launcher**, which controls all randomization — settings, seed and Archipelago connection — so there's no in-game login to fiddle with anymore.

> **⚠️ Heads-up: antivirus & Windows SmartScreen.** Windows SmartScreen or your antivirus may flag the launcher the first time you run it. **This is a false positive** — Windows simply doesn't recognise this brand-new app yet, and new unsigned programs are flagged by default until they build reputation, regardless of their contents. It is safe: click **"More info" → "Run anyway"**, and restore/allow the file if your antivirus quarantines it. The app has been submitted to Microsoft for review, and the warning clears automatically as more people download and use it. *(A code-signing certificate would clear it instantly but is a costly recurring expense we're holding off on for now.)*

## Headline changes

- **New Multiworld Launcher.** The launcher now owns everything: choose your settings, seed and (for multiworld) your server/slot/password right in the launcher, then launch straight into a hooked-up game. The old in-game title-screen connection and F1 AP-login panel are gone.
- **Standalone is a first-class mode.** Play solo with the *exact same* options the Archipelago world exposes — goal, quest categories, skill pool, shuffles, bonus/extra check categories, Collection and **Custom** goals.
- **Seed library.** Every standalone seed is its own world with its own characters. Start a fresh seed or load/delete a previous one; only that seed's characters show up in-game.
- **Full standalone tracker.** The launcher's **Locations** tab now shows the complete location universe for a solo run — every category, all difficulties, checked *and* unchecked — exactly like a connected Archipelago session.
- **Custom goals in standalone.** Build your own win condition (act bosses, ubers, super-uniques, bulk check targets, collection, gold) without needing a server.
- **HD graphics** activate by default.

## Fixes

- Quest/goal completion now fires correctly in standalone (previously some goals could never complete offline).
- No more check-flood on login.
- Fixed a crash on zone changes in Zone-Locking mode.
- Boss Shuffle no longer spawns un-killable / cinematic bosses that blocked progression.
- Separated trap rewards from DeathLink (receiving a trap no longer death-links others); incoming DeathLink now correctly kills.
- Version now displays correctly in-game and in every registration file.

## Credits

- **solida1987** — project lead, game systems, quest design, AP integration
- **ꓘicka** & **Zoë** — Archipelago logic (regions & rules)

A legal copy of Diablo II + Lord of Destruction is required to play.
Stable-2.0.0.title: Stable 2.0.0

Stable-2.1.0.description:
# Diablo II Archipelago — Stable 2.1.0

A big quality-of-life + randomization update on top of Stable 2.0.0.

## Randomization
- **Boss shuffle split into two independent toggles:**
  - **Super-unique shuffle** — named mini-bosses (Bishibosh, Rakanishu, Pindleskin…) appear as a different but always-killable type (data-file based).
  - **Act-boss shuffle** — Andariel / Duriel / Mephisto / Diablo / Baal take on each other's appearance, while staying killable and resolving their quests + death cinematics correctly.
- **Monster shuffle reworked** — every populated area now gets a genuinely different monster set (no area keeps its own pool by chance anymore). The secret Cow Level always keeps its cows, and never leaks them into other zones.
- **Shop, skill-requirement and item-requirement randomization** now run identically in **both** standalone and Archipelago worlds, applied to the game's data tables before launch with a small on-screen progress bar (and a clean reset when the game closes).

## Fixes
- **Skill points no longer lost / going to −1** on relog after a reset/reinvest (the reinvest is now clamped to the points actually available).
- **"Awesome Loot" reward now drops real gear** (Hell-tier boss treasure) instead of mostly gold/potions.
- **Items arrive almost instantly** after a check — the old multi-second delivery delays are gone.
- **Treasure-Cow event prompt** is shorter and its font size is configurable.

## Quality of life
- **Standalone "Received" list** — solo runs now show what each check granted (location → reward), just like an Archipelago game.
- **In-game "Book" button** on the movable tracker overlay — one click opens the F1 book.
- **Graphics (D2GL) settings in the launcher** — set fullscreen, window size, V-Sync, FPS caps and more before launch, instead of only the in-game menu.

## Launcher (Multiworld Launcher 2.3.0)
- **Antivirus resilience** — if Windows Defender deletes or blocks the mod files (a common false positive), the launcher now offers to **re-download the missing files** and to **add the game + launcher folders to Defender's exclusions** with one click, and proactively checks the exclusions on startup. (A signed build is still the long-term fix.)
Stable-2.1.0.title: Stable 2.1.0

Stable-2.2.0.description:
# Diablo II Archipelago - Stable 2.2.0

The headline of this build is a brand-new **Map tab** in the launcher, plus a round of gameplay fixes driven by tester feedback.

## New: graphical Map tab (launcher)

A maphack-style map for Diablo II, built right into the Multiworld Launcher and fed live by the mod:

- **Live collision map** that reveals itself as you explore, with a "you are here" player dot. The whole level shape appears the moment you enter an area.
- **Full area list - every area in all five acts**, shown immediately (even areas you have not entered yet). Each area is coloured **green (open)** or **red (locked)** from the live Zone-Explorer gate state - so the lock order matches exactly what the game enforces. Switch the view between **Normal / Nightmare / Hell**.
- **Per-area checklist** of every check in that area - kills, hunts, area-entry ("connecting") quests, waypoint activations and story quests - with a done / total count that ticks off as you complete them.
- **Object markers** for chests, shrines, waypoints, barrels and urns, with a toggle to hide them for a cleaner map.
- **Entrance / exit dots** that mark where you cross between areas (including cave mouths), plus a "Leads to" list of each area's connections, coloured by whether the target is open or locked.
- A **Map tab** now appears for every game in the launcher (a friendly placeholder is shown where a map is not available for that game yet).

## Fixes (gameplay)

- **Empty dungeon / cave floors fixed** - every area is now guaranteed to be populated with monsters, so Tower Cellar, Catacombs, Maggot Lair and similar floors are never empty.
- **Starting skills are now granted in Archipelago games** (previously only standalone runs received them).
- **Received set items can be equipped immediately** - no more save-and-quit before a set piece becomes usable.
- **Skill points above level 20 are no longer lost** when reloading a character.
- **Removed a phantom Valley of Snakes "clear" check** that could never be completed.
- **The Book button is now a true toggle** - one click opens the quest log, another click closes it.

## Launcher (Multiworld Launcher 2.4.0)

- The new graphical Map tab described above.
- Everything from Multiworld Launcher 2.3.0.

---

Thanks to the testers whose detailed reports drove this round of fixes. Keep the reports coming.
Stable-2.2.0.title: Stable 2.2.0

Stable-2.3.0.description:
# Diablo II Archipelago — Stable 2.3.0

A bug-fix release based on tester feedback. Thanks for the reports!

## Fixes

- **Skill pool now matches what your class can actually use.** Some skills only work for
  their own class (class-specific animations — Paladin Smite, Amazon javelin throws,
  Barbarian sequences, Druid shape-shift bites, Assassin kicks/claws — and the Assassin
  trap skills). You no longer receive those as cross-class checks you can't use. Every skill
  placed in your seed is usable, and your own class's signature skills are unlocked for free
  from the start. (This is why a Sorceress sees 184 obtainable skills and an Assassin sees
  more — the extras are her own free trap/martial-arts skills, not missing checks.)

- **Starting skills now arrive reliably in Archipelago games.** The hand-off to the game on
  launch was racing the game's start-up, so some of the 6 starting skills could be dropped.
  They now sync robustly, and a manual **Re-sync** button (next to Connect/Disconnect in the
  launcher) lets you re-request items and re-confirm checks at any time without disconnecting.

- **In-game version now reads correctly** (it was stuck showing "Stable 2.1.0").

- **Game install/update no longer fails partway** with "X of Y files failed to download."
  Updates now pull the packaged build directly, so a partial download self-heals instead of
  forcing you to delete the game and reinstall.

## Notes

- Existing Archipelago seeds remain compatible.
- If you already have a character mid-seed, your skills carry over.
Stable-2.3.0.title: Stable 2.3.0

Stable-2.4.0.description:
# Diablo II Archipelago — Stable 2.4.0

Another bug-fix release based on tester feedback. Thanks for the reports!

## Fixes

- **Nightmare and Hell quest checks now register correctly.** On a seed set to clear
  Nightmare or Hell, story, hunt and zone-clear quests were giving out no checks, and the
  in-game tracker showed the *Normal* difficulty's completion data while you were standing in
  a higher difficulty. The mod wasn't reading which difficulty you were actually in, so every
  quest check was being scored against Normal. It now follows your live difficulty: Nightmare
  and Hell checks fire, and the tracker shows the right data. A character that's already
  mid-run on Nightmare/Hell will catch up on the next quest tick — the checks you'd earned but
  weren't sent will arrive.

- **Save & quit no longer scrambles the stash tabs or the F1 overlay.** Logging out and back
  in could leave the stash tabs jumbled (showing the wrong AP/Shared set) or the F1 menu
  graphics glitched, and it only "fixed itself" after a full relaunch. The cleanup that runs
  when you leave a game wasn't always completing — two different exit detectors were racing and
  one could cut the other off, leaking the previous character's state into the next load. There
  is now a single, complete cleanup, so the stash and overlay come back correctly every time
  without a restart.

## Notes

- Existing Archipelago seeds remain compatible.
- Reminder: there are two separate stashes by design — your private AP stash during a run, and
  the Shared stash. The Personal (P) tab is always your own. With this release, which set is
  shown stays consistent after a save & quit.
- Heads-up for the curious: item level requirements still appear after you identify an item —
  that's intentional. Removing them would let any drop roll wildly over- or under-powered for
  your level and break the item progression, so it stays as-is.
Stable-2.4.0.title: Stable 2.4.0

Stable-2.5.0.description:
# Diablo II Archipelago — Stable 2.5.0

The stash redesign release, plus a batch of bug fixes from tester reports. Thanks for the testing!

## New: one chest

- **The stash is now a single chest** — no more separate "AP" and "Shared" sets that swapped around and showed
  whichever one they felt like. There is one continuous set of tabs (Personal "P", chest tabs 2–10, and the
  CON/RUN/GEM stackable tabs), shown the same way every time, in AP and standalone alike.

- **Stash isolation toggle.** New option **"Isolate stash to this seed"** (default ON):
  - **On** — the chest is isolated to the seed. Only characters in the same seed share it, so you can't carry
    unrelated items in from other games.
  - **Off** — the chest is global, shared with all your other "global" characters across every game.
  - Set it in the standalone randomize setup (under Goal & Mode), or in your YAML for Archipelago games
    (option "Isolate Stash To Seed", under Quality of Life).
  - Your existing stash carries over automatically — nothing is lost.

## New: shared progression across characters in a seed

- Make a **second character in the same seed** and it catches up to the seed's level automatically (and gets the
  matching stat and skill points), so you're not starting from level 1 when the seed is already deep in
  Nightmare or Hell. A character is only ever brought *up*, never down.
- If you'd rather keep characters fully separate, turn it off with `[settings] SeedSharedProgression=0` in
  `d2arch.ini`.

## Fixes

- **Stackable-tab item duplication fixed.** Putting a tome (or an arrow/bolt quiver) into the Consumables tab and
  pulling it back out could print extra copies. You now get back exactly what you put in.
- **F1 skill book no longer corrupts when you switch characters** in the same seed. (It only happened sometimes;
  the cause was a stale graphics handle when the game reused a character slot.)
- **Replacing a skill with a Reset Point now refunds the old skill's points** back to your pool to re-spend
  freely, instead of carrying them onto the new skill. (Takes effect after you log out and back in.)
- **New "Reset Skill Points" button** in the F1 skill book. Costs 3 Reset Points and refunds ALL your invested
  skill points (your chosen skills stay — only their points reset). Press it, then log out and back in within
  one minute to confirm; if you don't, nothing happens and no Reset Points are spent.

## Notes

- Existing Archipelago seeds and characters remain compatible.
- The earlier Nightmare/Hell quest-check fix and the save-and-quit stash/overlay fix (Stable 2.4.0) are included.
Stable-2.5.0.title: Stable 2.5.0

Stable-2.6.0.description:
# Diablo II Archipelago — Stable 2.6.0

A new opt-in trap plus a batch of fixes from the bug reports. Thanks for testing!

## New: Monster Revive Trap

- Optional trap you can switch on in your YAML (Archipelago) or in the standalone
  randomize setup. When it's on, a handful of monsters per game become "trapped" —
  kill a trapped monster and about a second later **8 hostile copies of it spawn**
  right where it died. A sudden ambush. Only normal monsters get trapped (never
  bosses or super-uniques), there's a per-game cap, and the copies can't trap-chain.
- Off by default. You'll find it under **Difficulty & XP** in the YAML, or as a
  checkbox in the standalone randomize setup.

## Fixes

- **F1 skill book graphics corruption fixed.** After playing for a while / switching
  characters, the skill book could start drawing garbled or random in-game graphics —
  buttons and skill icons turning into junk. That was a graphics-memory leak: the book
  reloaded its art on every character switch and never freed the old copies, so they
  piled up and eventually pointed at the wrong sprites. The art now loads once and stays
  put. The same fix was applied to the stash icons, which had the same problem.
- **"Item level requirements" toggle now works in Archipelago.** It worked in standalone
  but was being ignored in AP games — fixed.
- **Custom goals with a "complete all X quests" target could never finish.** The
  all-hunts / all-kills / all-waypoints / all-level / all-exploration / all-story
  subsystem targets were checking the wrong internal range, so the goal could sit at
  "almost done" forever even after you'd done everything. They now check the real quest
  list. If you used one of these, your goal completes properly now.
- **Character save reset hardened.** Extra safety checks so a bad skill-reset can never
  clear or corrupt a character — if anything looks wrong it leaves your save untouched
  and keeps the timestamped backups it already makes.

## Notes

- Existing Archipelago seeds and characters remain compatible.
- Everything from Stable 2.5.0 (one chest + stash isolation + shared progression) is included.

Stable-2.6.1.description:
# Diablo II Archipelago — Stable 2.6.1

Hotfix for issues reported on 2.6.0. Thanks for the quick reports!

## Fixes

- **Reward gold (and stat/skill points) no longer duplicate on reload.** Gold, stat
  points and skill points awarded by checks were being re-granted every time you
  reloaded the character — your totals kept ballooning, and the inflated gold made
  gold milestones / gold goals trigger far too early. Rewards are now delivered
  exactly once and saved into your character normally. This is the recurring "gold"
  problem.
- **Custom Goal no longer shows "Custom Goal Complete!" the moment you enter the
  game.** A Custom goal with no targets selected was being treated as instantly
  complete. An empty Custom goal now falls back to Full Normal (beat Baal on Normal)
  instead of auto-winning.
- **Collection goal had the same false-complete and is fixed too** — an empty
  Collection goal no longer reports complete with nothing collected.
- **Zone tracker shows proper super-unique names.** The F4 gate tracker was showing
  internal keys (e.g. "BishiMausol", "RakanishuUG", "PitspawnPit") — it now shows the
  real names (Bishibosh, Rakanishu, Pitspawn, …).

## Notes

- Everything from Stable 2.6.0 (Monster Revive Trap + the F1 / save-reset / custom-goal
  soft-lock fixes) is included.
- Existing seeds and characters remain compatible.
- The launcher is unchanged (still 2.7.0) — no launcher update needed.

# Diablo II Archipelago — Stable 2.6.2

## Item requirement options

- Item requirements are now **two independent options** — **Item Level Requirements**
  and **Item Stat Requirements**. Disable either to ignore that requirement when
  equipping gear: level requirements and strength/dexterity requirements can be turned
  off separately, and both apply across all gear (base items, magic/rare, set and

## Other

Stable-2.7.0.description:
# Diablo II Archipelago — Stable 2.7.0

## Zone Locking + Entrance Shuffle now work together

- **Zone Locking and Entrance Shuffle can finally be enabled at the same time**, in
  both Archipelago and standalone play. Previously turning on one forced the other
  off, because gate keys were earned by killing a specific super-unique in a fixed
  zone — and shuffling the entrances moved that boss out of reach.
- When **both** options are on, each gate key is now earned by completing a
  **condition** instead — collect gold, reach a character level, activate shrines,
  or (in Acts 3–5) break barrels/urns or open chests. These conditions don't depend
  on where an entrance leads, so the two systems no longer conflict.
- Conditions scale across the route and per difficulty (Normal / Nightmare / Hell),
  and early-act gates only use sources that are plentiful that early, so a key is
  always earnable with the areas you can already reach.
- The **F4 zone tracker** shows each gate's condition and your live progress
  (e.g. `Gold 3,200 / 5,000`) in place of the boss name, and the **launcher Map**
  turns a zone green the moment its condition is met.

## Received set & unique items

- Set and unique items received from Archipelago now arrive **unidentified**. Just
  identify them (Cain or an identify scroll) and they become normal items you can
  move and equip. This avoids the issue where moving a freshly-received set item
  could make it vanish unless you saved and reloaded first.

## Install check ("some files may be missing")

- Fixed the false **"some files may be missing. Consider re-installing"** warning that
  appeared on every launch. It was caused by an outdated file list being packed into
  the download, so the check compared your install against the wrong version — nothing
  was actually missing.
- The install check is also much more helpful now: instead of a vague warning it lists
  the **exact files** that are missing or the wrong size, and **automatically downloads
  and restores** them when it can. If a file genuinely can't be downloaded, it says so
  clearly.

## Notes

- Only the combined Zone Locking + Entrance Shuffle setup uses conditions — using
  either option on its own is unchanged.
- Gate keys are progression items, so Archipelago places them correctly across all
  worlds in a multiworld.
- Existing seeds and characters remain compatible.
- Launcher updated to 2.7.2.
Stable-2.7.0.title: Stable 2.7.0

Stable-2.8.0.description:
# Diablo II Archipelago — Stable 2.8.0

## Zone Locking + Entrance Shuffle — full key-condition tuning

- The combined Zone Locking + Entrance Shuffle key conditions are fully rebalanced.
  Two new condition types join the pool: **Kill X monsters** and **Find X
  super-uniques**.
- Every act's **final key is a monster-kill milestone** that climbs continuously
  through all difficulties (500 in Act 1 Normal up to 100,000 by the end of Hell).
  The other keys per act are a varied mix (level, gold, super-uniques, shrines,
  barrels, urns, chests) and no longer all the same type.
- All count conditions (monsters, shrines, barrels, urns, chests) are now **lifetime
  totals that count straight through Normal → Nightmare → Hell** — one number that
  keeps climbing, much easier to read.
- Thresholds are calibrated to the level you're expected to reach by the end of each
  act on normal XP, so keys arrive at a sensible pace.
- Barrels and urns only appear from Act 3 on Normal (too few earlier), but can appear
  on any act in Nightmare/Hell. Chests can appear on any act.

## XP bonus reworked

- The XP setting is now **0 = standard XP**, and each step adds **+100%** (1 = double,
  2 = triple, …). It now goes all the way up to **100**.

## Configurable gold & XP rewards

- Gold and XP rewards now roll a **random amount between a minimum and a maximum** you
  set. Each can range anywhere from 1 up to 1,000,000.

## Received set & unique items

- Set and unique items received from Archipelago arrive **unidentified** and now stay
  put reliably — identify them with Cain or a scroll and they're ready to use, no
  save-and-reload needed.

## Notes

- Existing seeds and characters remain compatible.
- Launcher updated to 2.8.0.
Stable-2.8.0.title: Stable 2.8.0

Stable-2.8.1.description:
# Diablo II Archipelago — Stable 2.8.1

A bug-fix release addressing tester feedback from 2.8.0.

## Gameplay fixes

- **XP rewards no longer wildly inflated.** A received XP reward was being run
  through the XP multiplier, so a 415k reward could pay out ~37 million. Flat
  rewards are now delivered exactly as rolled; the multiplier only affects XP
  you earn from kills.
- **Monsters spawn properly again.** The launcher was forcing every area to its
  largest ("Hell") size on all difficulties, which spread monsters paper-thin —
  floors like Tower Cellar and the Catacombs came up nearly empty with only the
  fixed champion/elite packs. Areas now keep their native per-difficulty size,
  so they populate normally.
- **Gold check now counts vendor sales.** Gold earned from selling items to a
  vendor was being ignored. It now counts toward the gold check — and the old
  drop-it-then-pick-it-up trick to farm the same gold no longer works.
- **Super-unique requirement lowered.** The Find-X-super-uniques key condition
  asked for more super-uniques than are reachable that early; the thresholds are
  reduced to attainable counts.
- **Boss loot fixed.** Boss-kill loot was collapsing to mostly potions because
  the drop roll was being seeded off the player instead of a monster. It now
  rolls a proper boss treasure class.

## Quality-of-life fixes

- **Stash isolation toggle now fully applies.** The per-seed vs. global shared-
  stash setting now also keys the serialized shared stash, so switching the
  toggle behaves consistently. Existing stashes migrate automatically.
- **Skill items respect your settings.** With Skill Hunting **off**, no skill
  "filler" items are placed at all (your character already gets every class
  skill at creation). With a **custom class filter**, only the classes you tick
  are used — leaving them all unticked no longer silently falls back to every
  class.
- **Class filter persists in single-player.** Your selected class filter is now
  saved and restored between sessions.
- **Correct set pieces delivered.** Received set items now arrive as the exact
  set piece intended instead of occasionally swapping to a different set item
  that shares the same base item.
- **F1 skill book no longer corrupts after a respec + save & quit.** Using a
  reset/respec and then saving and quitting could leave the F1 panel drawing
  garbage and crash on reopen. Its graphics now reload cleanly each session.

## Monster Revive Trap

- The trap's revived monsters now spawn in small staggered batches instead of
  all at once, which avoids the rendering crash some testers hit.

## Notes

- Existing seeds and characters remain compatible.
- Launcher updated to 2.8.1.
Stable-2.8.1.title: Stable 2.8.1

Stable-2.8.2.description:
# Diablo II Archipelago — Stable 2.8.2

A follow-up bug-fix release.

## Fixes

- **Isolated stash now actually isolates.** In Archipelago — especially
  multiworld — the per-seed stash isolation toggle silently fell back to the
  shared global stash, so you always saw the same chest regardless of the
  setting. It now keys correctly per AP game (using your slot when needed).
- **Shared seed progression works in multiworld.** The same underlying cause
  stopped the shared-seed XP catch-up from working in multiworld games (it
  worked in solo AP). Now fixed for both.
- **Deckard Cain town dialogue fixed.** Talking to rescued Cain back in the
  Act 1 town no longer wrongly awards the Act 3 Cain dialogue check.
- **Starting Skills is a YAML option again.** Multiworld was locked at 6
  starting skills while single-player had a slider — you can now set 0–10 in
  your YAML.

## Notes

- Launcher unchanged (still 2.8.1).
- Existing seeds and characters remain compatible.

Stable-2.8.3.description:
# Diablo II Archipelago — Stable 2.8.3

Critical crash hotfix.

## Fix

- **Fixed the crash after opening the F1 panel and then saving & quitting.**
  For a brief moment after you returned to the menu, the mod was still using the
  previous game's data (a freed/stale pointer) — the F1 panel kept drawing its
  graphics during that teardown, which crashed the renderer ("glider cache" /
  D2Glide) and could take the whole game down. The mod now detects the game exit
  immediately: it stops reading the old game and closes the F1 panel before it
  can draw stale graphics. This also stops the related background error spam.

## Notes

- Launcher unchanged (still 2.8.1).
- Existing seeds and characters remain compatible.

Stable-2.8.4.description:
# Diablo II Archipelago — Stable 2.8.4

Critical crash hotfix (follow-up to 2.8.3).

## Fix

- **Fixed the remaining crash after save & quit.** The 2.8.3 fix used the wrong
  signal to detect when the game had ended, so a crash could still happen in the
  brief moment after save & quit while the game was tearing down (the game data
  is freed but the mod's overlay/background-checks ran one more time on it). This
  release detects the exit with a reliable signal **and** wraps the in-game
  overlays and background checks in a safety net, so any stale read during that
  teardown moment is caught instead of taking the game down.

## Notes

- Launcher unchanged (still 2.8.1).
- Existing seeds and characters remain compatible.

Stable-2.8.5.description:
# Diablo II Archipelago — Stable 2.8.5

Crash-diagnostics overhaul + continued save & quit hardening.

## Changes

- **Much better crash logs.** Crash reports now stamp the **exact build version**,
  log each unique crash location **only once** (instead of repeating it thousands
  of times into a multi-megabyte file), and record whether the game was already
  shutting down plus the game/player state at the moment of the crash. This makes
  the remaining save & quit crash reports actually diagnosable instead of being
  buried in noise.
- Continued hardening of the in-game overlays and background checks against the
  save & quit teardown race (building on 2.8.4).

## If you still crash on save & quit

Please send **both** `d2arch_log.txt` and the new `d2arch_crash.txt`. The crash
file is now short and starts with the build version + state, which tells us
exactly where to fix it.

## Notes

- Launcher unchanged (still 2.8.1).
- Existing seeds and characters remain compatible.

Stable-2.8.6.description:
# Diablo II Archipelago — Stable 2.8.6

## Change

- **The game log now always starts with the build version.** `d2arch_log.txt`
  now writes the exact version (and session timestamp) as its **guaranteed first
  line**, the moment the mod loads — even if anything later fails. When a tester

Stable-2.8.7.description:
# Diablo II Archipelago — Stable 2.8.7

**The save & quit crash fix** (the real one).

## Fix

- **Fixed the crash after save & quit + entering another game.** Thanks to the
  new crash logs (2.8.5 / 2.8.6) we finally pinned down the real cause: after you
  saved & quit and started a fresh game, the mod kept using the **previous game's
  freed memory** because its internal game pointer only refreshed under specific
  conditions that don't happen on every setup. So running around in the new game
  (e.g. over to Akara) read freed memory and crashed. It now re-reads the live
  game **every tick**, so it always works on the current game — no more
  stale-memory crashes after a save & quit.

## Notes

- Launcher unchanged (still 2.8.1).
- Existing seeds and characters remain compatible.
Stable-2.8.7.title: Stable 2.8.7

Stable-2.8.8.description:
# Diablo II Archipelago — Stable 2.8.8

**The save & quit crash — real root-cause fix (round 2).**

## Fix

- **Fixed the crash after save & quit + entering another game.** 2.8.7 refreshed
  the internal game pointer once per game-tick, which closed *most* of the gap —
  but a freshly entered game is already live for a short window during character
  load + the first frames, *before* that first tick runs. Checks that fired in
  that window were still reading the **previous, freed game's memory**, which
  fed garbage into the game's own stat code and crashed (typically while running
  around right after re-entering). The mod now re-reads the live game **at the
  moment each check actually uses it**, not just once per tick — so it can never
  operate on a stale game, even during the load window. This was confirmed and
  fixed from a clean local crash log, not guesswork.

## Notes

- Launcher unchanged (still 2.8.1).
- Existing seeds and characters remain compatible.
- The F1 skill-book graphics fix from earlier builds is working as intended.
Stable-2.8.8.title: Stable 2.8.8

Stable-2.8.9.description:
# Diablo II Archipelago — Stable 2.8.9

**The save & quit HARD-LOCK fix.**

## Fix

- **Fixed a hard-lock (frozen client) after save & quit** — typically when
  opening the skill book / collection page again. The mod caches item and skill
  icons for speed, but a few of those caches were holding icon handles from the
  *previous* game after a save & quit, and Diablo II frees those when a game
  ends. Drawing a freed icon tripped a Diablo II internal assertion that froze
  the client (it couldn't be caught like a normal crash, which is why it locked
  instead of closing). Every icon cache now refreshes per game session and
  re-checks each icon right before drawing, so a stale icon is reloaded instead
  of drawn. Found and fixed from a clean local crash log plus a full audit of
  every icon cache in the mod.
- Note: this was *exposed* by the 2.8.8 crash fix — the game now survives long
  enough to reach this draw instead of crashing to desktop first.

## Notes

- Launcher unchanged (still 2.8.2).
- Existing seeds and characters remain compatible.
Stable-2.8.9.title: Stable 2.8.9

Stable-2.9.0.description:
# Diablo II Archipelago — Stable 2.9.0

Save & quit stability — the series of save & quit crash/lock fixes (2.8.3 → now)
rolled up into one stable release.

## Fix (this build)

- **Fixed a crash on the first save & quit back to the menu** when entrance
  shuffle is enabled. On the way out, the mod reverts the dungeon-size tweaks it
  applied at the start of the game. On some setups Diablo II frees that data
  *before* the revert runs, so the revert read freed memory and crashed the
  client. The revert is now fully guarded — if the data is already gone it is
  simply skipped (the next game reloads it fresh), so save & quit can't crash
  there anymore.

## Included since 2.8.2 (the save & quit series)

- Fixed the crash after save & quit + entering another game (stale game pointer,
  now re-read live at every use).
- Fixed the hard-lock after save & quit when opening the skill book / collection
  page (stale icon caches, now refreshed per game session + re-checked before
  drawing).

## Notes

- Launcher unchanged (still 2.8.2).
- Existing seeds and characters remain compatible.

Stable-2.9.1.description:
# Diablo II Archipelago — Stable 2.9.1

Crash-logging upgrade to catch the real cause of the remaining save & quit crash.

## Change

- **The crash log now captures non–access-violation faults too** — e.g. a Diablo
  II internal assertion, which is a *different kind* of fault than an access
  violation. Until now those were invisible: the log filled with **caught,
  non-fatal** access-violation entries (each one safely handled, the game kept
  running) while the *actual* crash left no record — so we kept chasing the wrong
  line. Access-violation entries are now clearly labeled "usually caught", and any
  fatal non-AV fault is logged with its exact fault code so it stands out.
- **If you still hit the save & quit crash, please send `d2arch_crash.txt`** — it
  should now show the real cause (look for a "NON-AV FAULT" line).

## Notes

- Includes all the save & quit stability fixes from 2.9.0 (stale game pointer,
  stale icon caches, entrance-shuffle maze-revert).
- Launcher unchanged (still 2.8.2). Existing seeds and characters compatible.
Stable-2.9.1.title: Stable 2.9.1

Stable-2.9.2.description:
﻿# Diablo II Archipelago — Stable 2.9.2

Crash-logging: now captures much more context for the remaining crash.

## Change
- The remaining rare crash (e.g. when switching characters after a while) is a
  Diablo II-internal exit, not a normal access violation, so previous logs caught
  nothing. d2arch_crash.txt now records, on every exit, a breadcrumb of which
  subsystem was running PLUS the tail of the live log (the exact last steps,
  including the character teardown/load sequence). If you hit it, please send
  d2arch_crash.txt — the new "SESSION END ... last-breadcrumb=" + log tail will
  show what was happening.

## Notes
- Includes all save & quit stability fixes from 2.9.x. The game is more stable;
  this build is mainly about capturing the cause of the last rare crash.
- Launcher unchanged (still 2.8.2).

Stable-2.9.3.description:
# Diablo II Archipelago — Stable 2.9.3

**The crash fix — found at last, and it wasn't the mod code.**

## Fix

- **Fixed the random crash** (`Diablo II Error — Assertion Failure … glTextureCache.cpp`)
  that some players hit after a while, especially around save & quit. The cause was the
  **bundled graphics renderer (d2gl)**: the version shipped was an older build whose
  texture cache asserts on certain graphics Diablo II sends it (the older renderer
  hard-crashes where the newer one doesn't). Updated the renderer (`ddraw.dll`) to the
  **official d2gl 1.3.3**, which fixes it. A tester confirmed: with the updated renderer,
  **no crashes across many save & quits**.

## Notes

- This updates one bundled file (the d2gl renderer). All the save & quit stability work
  from 2.8.7–2.9.0 still applies — those were real fixes; this final crash was the
  renderer, not the mod.
- Launcher unchanged (still 2.8.2). Existing seeds and characters remain compatible.
Stable-2.9.3.title: Stable 2.9.3

Stable-2.9.4.description:
﻿# Diablo II Archipelago — Stable 2.9.4

## Fix
- **Fixed the F1 skill-menu crash** (the "Diablo II Error / Assertion Failure" that
  happened when opening F1 and changing tabs). The editor was occasionally trying to
  draw a skill icon by an index that doesn''t exist in the icon sheet; the graphics
  renderer hard-crashes when handed a non-existent texture. The mod now refuses to draw
  an out-of-range icon instead of crashing. The F1 menu may still show some wrong icons
  in rare cases, but it will no longer crash the game.

## Notes
- Pairs with 2.9.3''s renderer update (d2gl 1.3.3). Together they close the crash issues.
- Launcher unchanged (2.8.2). Existing seeds/characters compatible.
Stable-2.9.4.title: Stable 2.9.4

Stable-2.9.5.description:
# Diablo II Archipelago — Stable 2.9.5

## Boss-loot rewards reworked

The "Awesome loot" boss rewards have been completely rebuilt so they actually drop **good** loot.

- **Each boss drops from its OWN loot table.** Andariel, Duriel, Mephisto, Diablo and Baal each pull from their real treasure-class item-level band, so higher bosses drop higher-tier bases (Andariel 39-69 … Baal 54-87).
- **Full quality spectrum, driven by Magic Find.** Every reward rolls across magic (blue) → rare (yellow) → set (green) → unique (gold), plus charms. Your Magic Find now actually works on it (using the game's real diminishing-returns caps), and even with **0 Magic Find** there is a solid floor — every item is at least magic quality, never white/junk.
- **Gold included.** Each reward also grants gold, scaled by the boss.
- **3-5 items per reward**, delivered immediately when the check completes — no delay. Items go straight to your inventory; if your inventory is full they drop on the ground instead, so you always get the full reward.
- Set/unique rewards arrive unidentified (identify them with Cain or a scroll) so they can no longer desync and disappear.

### Previous fix carried forward
- Renderer kept up to date (d2gl 1.3.3) and the F1 menu frame-bounds guard from 2.9.4 remain in place.

---

How to update: open the launcher and let it update, or reinstall from this release.
Stable-2.9.5.title: Stable 2.9.5

Stable-2.9.6.description:
# Diablo II Archipelago — Stable 2.9.6

## Goal-completion hotfix (Full Normal / Nightmare / Hell)

Fixed a bug where a Full Normal / Full Nightmare / Full Hell goal could fail to complete even after you killed the goal boss (Baal) on the required difficulty — no "Goal Complete" was sent to Archipelago.

Cause: the goal boss kill was being detected and recorded, but the win was only ever fired at the exact moment the quest transition was first seen. If that moment was missed — for example the boss quest was already complete when the character loaded (the load-time backfill marks it silently), or the goal data hadn't finished loading at that instant — the goal was marked done internally but the win never triggered, and there was no second chance.

Now the Full goals are re-checked every tick (the same safety net the Collection and Custom goals already had): the moment the goal boss is recorded complete on the goal difficulty, the win fires — on a fresh kill, on reconnect, or on the next login. Existing characters that already beat their goal boss will complete the goal as soon as they load in.

(Includes the boss-loot rework from 2.9.5.)
Stable-2.9.6.title: Stable 2.9.6

Stable-2.9.7.description:
# Diablo II Archipelago — Stable 2.9.7

Maintenance + cleanup release. Pairs with Multiworld Launcher 2.8.3.

## Changes
- **Removed the obsolete "I play an Assassin" option.** Class-locked skills (Assassin traps, the cross-class native-only skills) now auto-unlock for the owning class on their own, so the toggle is no longer needed. It is gone from both the Archipelago and standalone settings.
- **Standalone: "Monster Revive Trap" moved under the Traps section** in the settings dialog (it was previously sitting under Goal & Mode).
- **Standalone: fixed settings being reset when re-opening the dialog.** The gold/XP reward ranges, the Monster Trap toggle, and the "isolate stash to this seed" choice are no longer silently reverted to defaults on a second open.
- **Skill pool counter** now shows the effective in-game pool size instead of the raw 210.

## Verification
This build went through a full Archipelago + standalone logic audit: option mapping (apworld ↔ launcher ↔ game) is consistent both ways, items/locations are correctly named and classified, and the region/progression structure was confirmed across many generated multiworlds — including correct Normal → Nightmare → Hell ordering when mixed with other games.

Includes everything from 2.9.5 (boss-loot rework) and 2.9.6 (full-difficulty goal completion).
Stable-2.9.7.title: Stable 2.9.7

Stable-2.9.8.description:
# Diablo II Archipelago — Stable 2.9.8

Hotfix for item delivery in multiworld games.

## Fixed
- **Loot items no longer get lost when your inventory fills up.** Charms, random set/unique items, boss loot and quest-reward items are delivered into your inventory — and if your bags are full, the game now drops them on the floor for you to pick up, exactly like a normal monster drop. Previously, once your inventory filled (which happens fast when items pour in while you're off playing another game in a multiworld), the delivery queue got stuck and every item after the first was silently lost. This affected both Archipelago and standalone play.

Stat points, skill points, reset points, gold and experience were never affected — this only touched the item-spawn path.

Launcher is unchanged (still Multiworld Launcher 2.8.3).
Stable-2.9.8.title: Stable 2.9.8

Stable-2.9.9.description:
# Diablo II Archipelago — Stable 2.9.9

Item-delivery reliability fixes. Every reward you earn now actually reaches you.

## Fixes

- **Set / Unique gear from checks no longer disappears.** Random set and unique
  items delivered from a check could silently vanish instead of landing in your
  inventory. Delivery now uses the exact same reliable path the other rewards use
  and drops the extra identity handling that was desyncing the item. Charms, set
  and unique gear all arrive every time (and if the bags are full, the item drops
  on the floor next to you instead of being lost).

- **Boss-loot bonus now drops its full table.** The per-boss loot bonus was
  delivering far fewer items than intended — rare-quality rolls (and some set /
  unique rolls) were failing to spawn and quietly dropped, so a boss that should
  give 3–5 items often gave only 1–2. Every rolled item now falls back through
  rare → magic → normal until a real item is created, so each boss reliably drops
  its whole loot table with a proper mix of qualities.

## Notes

- No changes to the launcher this release — it stays on the current version.

beta-1.0.0.description:
# Diablo II Archipelago

A randomizer mod for Diablo II: Lord of Destruction (1.10f) with [Archipelago](https://archipelago.gg/) multiworld support.

Randomizes skill unlocks across a quest system spanning all 5 Acts. Complete quests to earn skills from any of the 7 character classes. Play solo with custom settings or connect to an Archipelago multiworld server for cross-game randomization.

---

## Features

- **210 Skills** from all 7 classes (Amazon, Sorceress, Necromancer, Paladin, Barbarian, Druid, Assassin) randomized into a quest reward pool
- **231 Quests** across 5 Acts: Story, Super Unique Hunting, Zone Clears, Exploration, Waypoints, Level Milestones
- **Skill Editor** (F1): Assign unlocked skills to your build with 3 tabs and 10 slots per tab
- **Quest Log** (F2): Track progress with Main/Side quest tabs, scrollbar, and per-difficulty tracking
- **Quest Tracker HUD** (F3): Shows current objectives and goal progress
- **Trap System**: Certain quests spawn dangerous Super Unique monsters near you
- **Reset Points**: Earned from quests, used to reassign skills
- **Tier System**: T1 (Level 1+), T2 (Level 20+), T3 (Level 40+) skill gating
- **Archipelago Integration**: Full multiworld support with item sending/receiving, DeathLink, and goal tracking
- **Configurable Settings**: Quest types, skill pool size, filler distribution, and more

---

## Installation

### Requirements
- Diablo II + Lord of Destruction (original installation required)
- Windows 10 or later

### Steps
1. Download the latest release ZIP from [Releases](https://github.com/solida1987/Diablo-II-Archipelago/releases)
2. Extract the ZIP anywhere on your computer
3. Run **D2ArchSetup.exe**
4. Click Browse and select your Diablo II installation folder
5. Click Install
6. Launch with **Play Archipelago.exe**

The installer copies required files from your Diablo II installation. Your original game is not modified.

---

## How to Play

### Singleplayer
1. Run **Play Archipelago.exe**
2. Select **Singleplayer**
3. Configure game settings (goal scope, quest types, skill pool, filler distribution)
4. Click **Play**
5. Create a new character or continue an existing one

### Archipelago Multiworld
1. Install the **.apworld** file (found in files/Archipelago/) into your Archipelago installation
2. Generate a multiworld with your YAML settings
3. Host or connect to an AP server
4. Run **Play Archipelago.exe**
5. Select **Archipelago**
6. Enter server address, slot name, and password
7. Click **Play**

The console window shows AP connection status and events in real-time.

---

## Controls

| Key | Action |
|-----|--------|
| F1 | Open/Close Skill Editor |
| F2 | Open/Close Quest Log |
| F3 | Toggle Quest Tracker HUD |
| ESC | Close any open panel |

---

## AP World Options (YAML)

| Option | Type | Default | Description |
|--------|------|---------|-------------|
| goal_scope | Choice | full_game | Act 1 only / Acts 1-2 / Acts 1-3 / Acts 1-4 / Full Game |
| difficulty_scope | Choice | normal_only | Normal / Normal+Nightmare / All Three |
| quest_story | Toggle | true | Include story quests |
| quest_hunting | Toggle | true | Include Super Unique hunting quests |
| quest_kill_zones | Toggle | true | Include zone clear quests |
| quest_exploration | Toggle | true | Include area entry quests |
| quest_waypoints | Toggle | true | Include waypoint quests |
| quest_level_milestones | Toggle | true | Include level milestone quests |
| skill_pool_size | 20-210 | 210 | Number of skills in the item pool |
| starting_skills | 1-20 | 6 | Skills unlocked at start |
| filler_gold_pct | 0-100 | 30 | Gold filler weight |
| filler_stat_pts_pct | 0-100 | 15 | Stat point filler weight |
| filler_skill_pts_pct | 0-100 | 15 | Skill point filler weight |
| filler_trap_pct | 0-100 | 15 | Trap filler weight |
| filler_reset_pts_pct | 0-100 | 25 | Reset point filler weight |
| starting_gold | 0-50000 | 0 | Starting gold amount |
| death_link | Toggle | false | Enable DeathLink |

---

## Built With

- [D2MOO](https://github.com/nicodoctor/D2MOO) - Diablo II open-source reimplementation for D2Common, D2Game, and Fog DLLs
- [D2.Detours](https://github.com/nicodoctor/D2.Detours) - DLL patching and injection framework
- [cnc-ddraw](https://github.com/FunkyFr3sh/cnc-ddraw) - Graphics wrapper for windowed mode on modern Windows
- [Archipelago](https://archipelago.gg/) - Cross-game multiworld randomizer framework

## Credits

- **solida** - Project lead, game systems, quest design, AP integration
- **D2MOO Team** - Open-source Diablo II reimplementation
- **Archipelago Community** - Multiworld framework and support
- **Diablo II Modding Community** - Research, tools, and documentation

## License

This project is a modification for Diablo II: Lord of Destruction. A legal copy of the original game is required to play.

beta-1.5.1.description:
## Diablo II Archipelago beta-1.5.1

Bugfix release based on full codebase audit. 12 bugs fixed.

### Critical Fixes
- **AP Bridge**: Fixed WebSocket dependency missing in packaged build (No module named websockets.asyncio)
- **Save Path**: Fixed save directory detection failing silently - now has 4-level fallback chain with logging
- **SuperUnique Hunts**: Fixed Corpsefire and other hunt kills not registering - added UNITFLAG_ISDEAD check
- **AP Reconnect**: Fixed filler items (gold, stat points, traps) duplicating on reconnect
- **Quest ID Collision**: Fixed duplicate quest ID 57 (Hole Level 1 vs Catacombs Waypoint) - 4 missing locations added

### Gameplay Fixes
- **Singleplayer Sync**: Gold, stat points, and skill points now show immediately instead of requiring save-exit
- **XP Multiplier**: Fixed counterproductive ExpRatio modification - now correctly divides level thresholds only
- **Assassin Weapons**: Katars (h2h) removed from shops to prevent crash when equipped by non-Assassin classes
- **Skill Points**: Fixed skill points jumping to wrong skills after reload - reinvest now validates against current tree
- **Boss Shuffle**: Improved randomization - retry mechanism ensures 75%+ of bosses actually change position

### Technical
- Dead monster tracking: circular buffer instead of full reset on overflow
- AP items_received_index: persistent to disk, prevents item loss on bridge restart
- Forward declaration fix for Log() function (compile error prevention)
- Full codebase audit documentation in Research/FULL_CODEBASE_MAP.md

### Installation
1. Download and extract the ZIP below
2. **Run D2ArchSetup.exe as Administrator**
3. Browse to your Diablo II folder and click Install
4. Launch with **Play Archipelago.exe**
